channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump’s Private Ballroom Project May Result in $1 Billion Cost to US Taxpayers
item.description: A controversial ballroom project associated with former President Donald Trump, which was promoted as being privately funded, may ultimately cost U.S. taxpayers up to $1 billion. The project has faced...

item.title: Emerging singer Caleb Burke faces criticism for debut music amid brother's murder case
item.description: Caleb Burke, an emerging singer from the US, has faced public backlash after releasing music under the name Kova while his brother, D4vd, is in custody facing serious charges. The controversy erupted ...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee Reaffirms She Will Not Resign Amid Political Pressure
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has reiterated her firm stance that she will not resign from her post despite increasing political pressure and speculation surrounding her government. Speak...

item.title: Finance Minister Flags Johannesburg’s Budget Crisis and Unfunded Wage Deal
item.description: South Africa’s Finance Minister, Enoch Godongwana, has raised serious concerns over the financial health of Johannesburg. In a letter dated 24 April 2026, he warned Mayor Dada Morero that the City is ...

item.title: Tanzanite Necklace from Mererani Shines at Met Gala 2026
item.description: In May 2026, Tanzania gained significant attention on the global luxury gemstone stage after Indian billionaire Sudha Reddy displayed a rare tanzanite necklace at the Met Gala in New York. The necklac...

item.title: Iran tells ships they need permission to cross the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran’s navy warned vessels near the Strait of Hormuz that they must obtain Tehran’s permission before transiting the critical waterway. According to the Wall Street Journal reporting behind the item, ...

item.title: Three Cruise Ship Passengers Evacuated Following Hantavirus Detection
item.description: South African health authorities have confirmed the presence of the Andes strain of hantavirus in two passengers from a cruise ship currently experiencing an outbreak of this rare viral infection. Hea...

item.title: Final Day of Campaigning Ahead of Elections in England, Scotland and Wales
item.description: Political parties across England, Scotland and Wales are making their final appeals to voters ahead of key elections on 7 May. In Scotland, the Scottish National Party aims to secure a fifth consecuti...

item.title: Telangana leaders request one-time age relaxation for government job aspirants
item.description: In Telangana, concerns have arisen regarding candidates who missed opportunities for government employment due to prolonged gaps in recruitment. Congress MLC M. Kodandaram and Government Whip Addanki ...

item.title: House Justice Committee Recommends Impeachment of VP Sara Duterte over Alleged Misuse of Funds and Threats
item.description: The House justice committee has recommended the impeachment of Vice President Sara Duterte, urging her removal from office and perpetual disqualification from holding any public position. The committe...

item.title: UCT Plans R250-Million Rebuild of Historic Jagger Library After 2021 Fire
item.description: The University of Cape Town (UCT) is moving ahead with plans to rebuild its iconic Jagger Library, which was destroyed in a fire on Table Mountain in April 2021. The building, a key research hub housi...

item.title: Government Subsidy on Diesel Introduced Amid Rising Fuel Prices in Tanzania
item.description: Fuel prices in Tanzania have gone up sharply this week, with petrol, diesel, and kerosene all seeing major increases. The government has stepped in to subsidise diesel at Sh259 per litre, mainly to he...

item.title: Kerala Voters Express Discontent Over Current State of Left Politics
item.description: The political landscape in Kerala has witnessed a notable shift as the electorate expressed disapproval, not necessarily of the Left ideology itself, but of the way the party has evolved over time. Ob...

item.title: Challenges in Controlling Malaria Despite Vaccines, Drugs and Mosquito Control
item.description: Malaria, caused by the Plasmodium parasite, remains a major health challenge in Africa despite decades of efforts using diagnostics, drugs, vaccines, and mosquito control measures. The parasite is ext...

item.title: Uganda passes revised law limiting foreign influence after economic warnings
item.description: Uganda's parliament has passed a revised 'Protection of Sovereignty Bill' aimed at limiting foreign influence in domestic politics, after the central bank warned that the original version could trigge...

item.title: Understanding the High Cost and Fast Usage of Mobile Data in Nigeria
item.description: For Nigerians, mobile data don become serious wahala because e dey cost wella and e dey finish sharp sharp. One major reason na say telecom towers dey run mainly on diesel generators due to constant w...

item.title: Congressional Debate Raises Questions About Trump’s Mental Fitness
item.description: The article examines recent developments in U.S. politics regarding President Donald Trump’s mental stability, particularly in the context of the ongoing conflict with Iran. Patrick Lawrence highlight...

item.title: CNN founder Ted Turner dies aged 87, remembered for media innovation and philanthropy
item.description: Ted Turner, the outspoken media pioneer and billionaire who founded CNN in 1980, has passed away at the age of 87. Turner, known for his bold personality and adventurous lifestyle, not only revolution...

item.title: Minister Tau: Sedibeng region attracting investment ahead of SEZ launch
item.description: Trade, Industry and Competition Minister Parks Tau has highlighted strong investor interest in the Sedibeng region, even before the government’s Special Economic Zone (SEZ) officially starts operation...

item.title: Commercial Shipping in Strait of Hormuz Disrupted Despite Technical Opening
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains technically open, but commercial shipping has not returned to normalcy, says Anil Devli, CEO of the Indian National Shipowners’ Association. With 14 Indian vessels trapped...
